args.description: Path(s) to list. Defaults to current directory.

flags.-l: Long listing format: shows permissions, owner, size, date
flags.-a: Show all files including hidden (starting with .)
flags.-t: Sort by modification time, newest first
flags.-S: Sort by file size, largest first
flags.-d: List directories themselves, not their contents
flags.-i: Print the index number (inode) of each file

grep.description: Search text using patterns (Global Regular Expression Print)

args.description: PATTERN followed by FILE(s) to search in

flags.-v: Invert match — show lines that do NOT match
flags.-c: Count the number of matching lines
flags.-l: Print only filenames of files with matches
flags.-o: Print only the matched part of the line
flags.-A: Show N lines After each match
flags.-B: Show N lines Before each match
flags.-C: Show N lines of Context around each match
flags.-q: Quiet mode — no output, just exit code

find.description: Search for files in a directory hierarchy

args.description: Starting directory for the search

flags.-type: Filter by type: f=file, d=directory, l=symlink
flags.-mtime: Modified N days ago (+N older than, -N within N days)
flags.-exec: Execute a command on each found file
flags.-maxdepth: Limit search depth to N levels
flags.-user: Files owned by a specific user
flags.-print: Print the full path of found files (default)

args.description: MODE followed by FILE(s) to change

flags.-R: Apply recursively to directories and their contents
flags.-c: Report only when a change is made
flags.u+x: Add execute permission for the owner (user)
flags.g+w: Add write permission for the group
flags.a+r: Add read permission for all (user, group, others)
flags.755: Owner: rwx, Group: r-x, Others: r-x
flags.644: Owner: rw-, Group: r--, Others: r--
flags.777: Full permissions for everyone (use with caution!)

args.description: Archive file followed by source file(s)/directory

flags.-f: Specify the archive filename (must be followed by filename)
flags.-t: List the contents of an archive
flags.-r: Append files to the end of an archive
flags.-p: Preserve file permissions

curl.description: Transfer data from or to a server using various protocols

flags.-o: Write output to a file instead of stdout

flags.-d: Send data in a POST request body
flags.-v: Verbose output — show request and response details
flags.-s: Silent mode — suppress progress and errors
flags.-k: Allow insecure SSL connections (skip cert validation)

ssh.description: OpenSSH — secure shell remote login client

args.description: [user@]hostname — the remote host to connect to

flags.-p: Port number to connect to on the remote host
flags.-N: Do not execute a remote command (useful for tunneling)
flags.-f: Go to background before command execution

args.description: Subcommand and arguments (e.g., run IMAGE, ps, build PATH)

flags.run: Create and start a container from an image
flags.ps: List running containers (-a to show all)
flags.build: Build an image from a Dockerfile
flags.pull: Pull an image from a registry
flags.push: Push an image to a registry
flags.exec: Run a command in a running container
flags.-d: Detached mode — run container in background
flags.-p: Publish container port to host: -p host:container
flags.--name: Assign a name to the container
flags.--rm: Automatically remove container when it exits

awk.description: Pattern scanning and text processing language

flags.-v: Assign a variable before execution (e.g., -v x=5)
flags.-f: Read the awk program from a file
flags.$1: First field of the current line
flags.$NF: Last field of the current line
flags.NR: Number of records (lines) processed so far
flags.NF: Number of fields in the current record
flags.BEGIN: Block executed before processing any lines
flags.END: Block executed after processing all lines

sed.description: Stream editor for filtering and transforming text

flags.-n: Suppress automatic printing of pattern space
flags.-e: Add a script/expression to be executed
flags.-i: Edit files in-place (optionally with backup suffix)
flags.s/old/new/: Substitute first occurrence of old with new

ps.description: Report a snapshot of current running processes

flags.-u: Show processes for a specific user

flags.-15: SIGTERM — politely ask process to terminate (default)

args.description: SOURCE followed by DESTINATION

flags.-v: Verbose — explain what is being done

flags.-p: Preserve timestamps, mode, and ownership

flags.-v: Verbose — explain what is being done

flags.-u: Move only when SOURCE is newer than DESTINATION

args.description: FILE(s) or DIRECTORY(s) to remove

flags.-r: Remove directories and their contents recursively

flags.-E: Show $ at end of each line

echo.description: Display a line of text or variable value

args.description: STRING(s) to print to stdout

flags.-n: Do not output the trailing newline

flags.-i: Wait N seconds between sending each packet

args.description: Optional filter arguments

flags.-p: Show PID/program name for sockets

df.description: Report file system disk space usage

args.description: FILE — check the filesystem this file is on

flags.-i: Show inode information instead of block usage

du.description: Estimate file and directory space usage

args.description: FILE(s) or DIRECTORY to measure

flags.-s: Summary only — total size of each argument
flags.-a: Show sizes for all files, not just directories

top.description: Display Linux tasks and system resource usage in real-time

args.description: No required arguments — runs interactively

flags.-p: Monitor only specific PID(s)
flags.-u: Show processes for a specific user
flags.-b: Batch mode — useful for output to file

head.description: Output the first part of files

args.description: FILE(s) to read from

tail.description: Output the last part of files

args.description: FILE(s) to read from

flags.-f: Follow: keep reading as file grows (great for logs!)
flags.-F: Like -f but keep retrying if file is inaccessible

wc.description: Print line, word, and byte counts for files

args.description: FILE(s) to count

flags.-L: Print the length of the longest line

args.description: FILE(s) to sort

flags.-n: Compare according to string numerical value

flags.-o: Write result to FILE instead of stdout

args.description: INPUT FILE (usually from sort)

flags.-c: Prefix lines by number of occurrences

flags.-n: Use at most N arguments per command line

flags.-p: Prompt before running each command line
flags.-r: Do not run command if stdin is empty

args.description: OWNER[:GROUP] followed by FILE(s)

flags.-c: Report only when a change is made
flags.--from: Only change if current owner matches given owner
flags.--reference: Use another file's ownership as reference

args.description: TARGET followed by LINK_NAME

flags.-s: Create a symbolic (soft) link instead of a hard link
flags.-n: Treat LINK_NAME as a normal file if it is a symlink to a dir
flags.-r: Create symbolic links relative to link location
flags.-b: Make a backup of each existing destination file

cron.description: Daemon to execute scheduled commands (crontab entries)

args.description: No direct arguments — managed via crontab -e

flags.weekday: 0-7: day of the week (0 and 7 = Sunday)

args.description: Optional cron file to install

flags.-e: Edit the current user's crontab in $EDITOR
flags.-u: Operate on another user's crontab (requires root)

args.description: URL(s) to download

flags.-O: Save file with a different name
flags.-o: Log all messages to a file instead of stderr
flags.-np: No parent — do not ascend to parent directory in recursive mode
flags.-P: Save files to a specific directory prefix
flags.-b: Go to background immediately after startup

rsync.description: Fast, versatile file copying and synchronisation tool

args.description: SOURCE and DESTINATION (local or user@host:path)

flags.-a: Archive mode: preserves permissions, timestamps, symlinks, owner (-rlptgoD)
flags.-n: Dry run — show what would be transferred without doing it
flags.-P: Show progress and keep partial files (combines --progress --partial)
flags.--delete: Delete files in destination that do not exist in source

flags.--checksum: Use checksum comparison instead of timestamp/size

flags.--ignore-existing: Skip files that already exist on destination

flags.-P: Port to connect to on the remote host

flags.-a: Append to files instead of overwriting

flags.-d: Use DELIM as the field delimiter instead of TAB

args.description: No required arguments

flags.-p: Print processor type

flags.-a: Print all matching executables in PATH, not just the first

flags.-i: List network files (use -i :PORT to filter by port)

args.description: Git subcommand (commit, push, pull, clone, etc.)

flags.commit: Record staged changes as a new snapshot in history
flags.push: Upload local commits to a remote repository
flags.pull: Fetch from remote and merge into current branch
flags.clone: Clone a remote repository into a new directory
flags.checkout: Switch branches or restore working tree files
flags.merge: Join two or more development histories together
flags.rebase: Reapply commits on top of another base tip
flags.diff: Show changes between commits or working tree
flags.add: Stage file changes for the next commit
flags.revert: Create a new commit that undoes a previous commit
flags.cherry-pick: Apply the changes from a specific commit
flags.bisect: Binary search through history to find a bad commit
flags.init: Initialise a new empty Git repository
flags.rm: Remove files from the working tree and index
flags.show: Show a Git object (commit, tag, tree, blob)
flags.-a: Automatically stage all tracked modified files
flags.-b: Create and switch to a new branch
flags.--amend: Modify the most recent commit (message or content)
flags.--rebase: Rebase instead of merge when pulling
flags.--force: Force push, overwriting remote history (use carefully!)
flags.--hard: Reset index and working tree (all local changes lost)
flags.--soft: Reset HEAD only, keep staged changes
flags.--mixed: Reset HEAD and index, keep working tree (default)
flags.--no-ff: Create a merge commit even for fast-forward merges
flags.--squash: Squash all commits into one before merging
flags.--oneline: Show each commit as a single line
flags.--graph: Draw an ASCII graph of the branch and merge history
flags.-p: Show patch (diff) output
flags.-u: Set upstream tracking branch (push -u origin main)
flags.--depth: Shallow clone with specified commit depth
flags.config: Get or set repository or global configuration values
flags.clean: Remove untracked files from the working tree
flags.reflog: Show a log of all HEAD movements — essential for recovery
flags.worktree: Manage multiple working trees from one repository
flags.submodule: Manage submodules embedded in the repository
flags.shortlog: Summarise git log output grouped by author
flags.archive: Export a tree or commit as a tar/zip archive
flags.blame: Show which commit last modified each line of a file
flags.-D: Force-delete a branch even if not fully merged
flags.-f: Force — overwrite branch tip or overwrite ref
flags.--force-with-lease: Safer force push — fails if remote has new commits you haven't fetched
flags.--fixup: Create a fixup! commit to be squashed by rebase --autosquash
flags.--autosquash: Automatically squash fixup!/squash! commits during rebase -i
flags.--global: Apply config to the global (~/.gitconfig) scope
flags.--local: Apply config to the repository (.git/config) scope

terraform.description: Infrastructure as Code tool by HashiCorp

args.description: Terraform subcommand (init, plan, apply, destroy, etc.)

flags.init: Initialise a Terraform working directory (downloads providers)
flags.plan: Preview changes Terraform will make to infrastructure
flags.apply: Apply the planned changes to real infrastructure
flags.destroy: Destroy all resources managed by Terraform
flags.validate: Validate configuration files for syntax errors
flags.fmt: Reformat configuration files to canonical style
flags.output: Show output values from the state
flags.import: Import existing infrastructure into Terraform state
flags.taint: Mark a resource as tainted, forcing recreation on next apply
flags.untaint: Remove the tainted state from a resource
flags.providers: Show required providers and their versions
flags.refresh: Update state to match real infrastructure
flags.graph: Output dependency graph in DOT format
flags.show: Show the current state or a plan file
flags.-auto-approve: Skip interactive approval prompt on apply/destroy
flags.-var: Set a single input variable value (-var 'key=value')
flags.-var-file: Load variable values from a .tfvars file
flags.-out: Save plan to a file for later apply
flags.-target: Focus apply/plan on a specific resource
flags.-reconfigure: Reconfigure the backend, ignoring any saved config
flags.-upgrade: Upgrade provider plugins to newest acceptable version
flags.-compact-warnings: Show warnings in a more compact format
flags.-lock-timeout: Duration to retry acquiring state lock

args.description: kubectl subcommand (get, apply, describe, delete, etc.)

flags.apply: Apply a configuration to a resource from a file or stdin
flags.delete: Delete resources by filenames, resource names, or labels
flags.describe: Show detailed state of one or more resources
flags.create: Create a resource from a file or stdin
flags.edit: Edit a resource in the default editor
flags.exec: Execute a command inside a running container
flags.logs: Print logs from a container in a pod
flags.port-forward: Forward local port(s) to a pod
flags.scale: Scale a Deployment, ReplicaSet, or StatefulSet
flags.patch: Update fields of a resource using JSON/YAML patch
flags.label: Add or update labels on a resource
flags.annotate: Add or update annotations on a resource
flags.expose: Expose a resource as a new Kubernetes service
flags.run: Run a particular image in the cluster
flags.cp: Copy files between a container and local filesystem
flags.top: Show CPU and memory usage of nodes or pods
flags.drain: Safely evict all pods from a node for maintenance
flags.taint: Update taints on one or more nodes
flags.-o: Output format: wide, json, yaml, jsonpath, custom-columns
flags.-f: Filename, directory, or URL of manifest file
flags.--dry-run: Preview changes without applying (client or server)
flags.--force: Immediately remove resources without graceful deletion
flags.--grace-period: Period in seconds before forceful termination
flags.--watch: Watch for changes to the listed resources
flags.--tail: Number of lines of log output to show
flags.--previous: Print logs from previous container instance
flags.--record: Record current command in the resource annotation
flags.wait: Wait until a specific condition is met on a resource
flags.api-resources: List all supported API resource types in the cluster
flags.api-versions: List all supported API versions in the cluster
flags.explain: Show documentation for a resource field (e.g., kubectl explain pod.spec)
flags.proxy: Run a proxy to the Kubernetes API server on localhost
flags.certificate: Approve or deny pending certificate signing requests
flags.--server-side: Perform server-side apply (more powerful conflict detection)
flags.--prune: Automatically delete resources missing from the applied manifests
flags.--cascade: Control cascade deletion of dependent resources (background/foreground/orphan)
flags.--for: Condition to wait for with kubectl wait (e.g., --for=condition=Ready)
flags.--timeout: Maximum time to wait before giving up (e.g., --timeout=60s)
flags.--output: Same as -o: control output format (json, yaml, wide, name, jsonpath…)
flags.--selector: Same as -l: filter resources by label selector
flags.--show-labels: Show all labels as the last column in output
flags.--sort-by: Sort list results by a JSONPath expression
flags.--field-selector: Filter resources by field value (e.g., status.phase=Running)

npm.description: Node Package Manager — manage JavaScript dependencies

args.description: npm subcommand (install, run, build, publish, etc.)

flags.install: Install all dependencies listed in package.json
flags.uninstall: Remove a package from node_modules and package.json
flags.update: Update packages to their latest allowed version
flags.run: Run a script defined in package.json scripts section
flags.start: Run the start script (default: node server.js)
flags.publish: Publish a package to the npm registry
flags.link: Symlink a local package for development
flags.pack: Create a tarball of the package
flags.--save: Save to dependencies in package.json (default in npm 5+)

systemctl.description: Control the systemd system and service manager

args.description: Subcommand followed by unit name (e.g., start nginx)

flags.restart: Stop and then start one or more units

flags.enable: Enable a unit to start at boot
flags.disable: Disable a unit from starting at boot

flags.status: Show runtime status and recent logs of a unit

flags.mask: Mask a unit (prevent it from being started at all)

flags.reset-failed: Reset the failed state of all (or specified) units

flags.--no-pager: Do not pipe output to a pager
flags.-l: Show full log lines without truncation

args.description: JQ filter expression followed by JSON file or piped input

Identity filter — output the input unchanged
flags..field: Extract value of a named object field
flags..[]: Iterate over all values in an array or object
flags..[0]: Extract element at index 0 of an array
flags.|: Pipe: pass output of left filter to right filter
flags.,: Produce multiple outputs from one input
flags.select(expr): Pass through values for which the expression is true
flags.map(expr): Apply a filter to each element of an array
flags.keys: Return the keys of an object as a sorted array
flags.values: Return the values of an object as an array
flags.has(key): Return true if the object has the given key
flags.in: Test if a value is a key in an object
flags.length: Return the length of an array, string, or object
flags.type: Return the type of a value (string, number, array…)
flags.not: Logical negation of a boolean filter
flags.to_entries: Convert an object to an array of {key, value} pairs
flags.from_entries: Convert an array of {key, value} pairs to an object
flags.add: Sum an array of numbers, or concatenate strings/arrays
flags.any: Return true if any element satisfies the condition
flags.all: Return true if all elements satisfy the condition
flags.group_by: Group array elements by a field value
flags.sort_by: Sort array elements by a field value
flags.unique_by: Remove duplicates by a field value
flags.first: Return the first output of an expression
flags.last: Return the last output of an expression
flags.limit(n;expr): Return at most n outputs from the expression
flags.env: Return the environment variables as a JSON object
flags.-r: Raw output — print strings without JSON quoting
flags.-c: Compact output — one JSON object per line
flags.-n: Null input — do not read input, use 'null' as input
flags.-e: Exit with non-zero status if the output is false/null
flags.-s: Slurp — read all input into a single array
flags.--arg: Pass a shell variable as a named jq string variable
flags.--argjson: Pass a shell value as a named jq JSON variable
flags.--slurpfile: Read a file and bind its contents as a jq variable
flags.--tab: Indent output using tabs instead of spaces

openssl.description: OpenSSL — cryptography toolkit and TLS/SSL library CLI

args.description: OpenSSL subcommand (genrsa, req, x509, s_client, enc, etc.)

flags.genpkey: Generate a private key (supports RSA, EC, Ed25519)
flags.req: Create a certificate signing request (CSR) or self-signed cert

flags.enc: Encrypt or decrypt a file using a symmetric cipher
flags.dgst: Compute hash digests (MD5, SHA-256, SHA-512, etc.)
flags.ca: Create and sign certificates as a CA
flags.-newkey: Generate a new private key of the specified type/size
flags.-x509: Output a self-signed certificate instead of a CSR
flags.-subj: Subject name in one line (avoids interactive prompts)
flags.-nodes: Do not encrypt the private key (no DES)
flags.-noout: Do not output the encoded certificate or key
flags.-text: Print the certificate in human-readable text form
flags.-servername: Set SNI hostname for TLS connection

flags.up: Create and start containers defined in docker-compose.yml

flags.exec: Run a command inside a running service container
flags.run: Run a one-off command on a service
flags.pull: Pull service images from their registries
flags.push: Push service images to their registries
flags.config: Validate and view the merged Compose configuration
flags.scale: Set the number of containers for a service
flags.kill: Force stop service containers with a signal
flags.-d: Detached mode — run containers in background
flags.--force-recreate: Recreate containers even if config hasn't changed
flags.--volumes: Remove named volumes declared in Compose file (with down)
flags.--remove-orphans: Remove containers for services not in the Compose file
flags.--tail: Number of log lines to show from the end
flags.--no-build: Don't build images, even if they're missing
flags.--abort-on-container-exit: Stop all containers if any container stops

args.description: Service and subcommand (e.g., aws s3 ls, aws ec2 describe-instances)

flags.s3: S3 object storage commands (ls, cp, mv, rm, sync, mb, rb)
flags.ec2: Elastic Compute Cloud — manage instances, AMIs, security groups
flags.iam: Identity and Access Management — users, roles, policies
flags.ecs: Elastic Container Service — run Docker containers at scale
flags.eks: Elastic Kubernetes Service — managed Kubernetes clusters
flags.ecr: Elastic Container Registry — private Docker image registry
flags.dynamodb: Managed NoSQL key-value and document database
flags.sts: Security Token Service — assume roles, get caller identity
flags.ssm: Systems Manager — parameter store, session manager
flags.logs: CloudWatch Logs — log groups, streams, insights
flags.configure: Set up AWS credentials and default region/output
flags.--output: Output format: json, yaml, text, table
flags.--query: JMESPath query to filter the output
flags.--endpoint-url: Override the service endpoint URL (useful for LocalStack)
flags.--debug: Show detailed debug output and HTTP calls
flags.--color: Toggle colorized output: on, off, auto
flags.--cli-input-json: Read parameters from a JSON file

args.description: Service group and command (e.g., az vm list, az group create)

flags.login: Authenticate to Azure (browser or service principal)
flags.storage: Manage storage accounts, blobs, queues, and tables
flags.network: Manage virtual networks, subnets, NICs, and NSGs
flags.webapp: Manage Azure App Service web apps
flags.keyvault: Manage Azure Key Vault secrets, keys, and certificates
flags.policy: Manage Azure Policy assignments and definitions
flags.monitor: Manage metrics, alerts, logs, and diagnostics
flags.devops: Manage Azure DevOps pipelines and repos
flags.servicebus: Manage Azure Service Bus namespaces and queues
flags.configure: Configure az CLI defaults (location, group, etc.)
flags.list: List resources of a given type
flags.show: Show details of a specific resource
flags.start: Start a VM or other stoppable resource
flags.restart: Restart a VM or web app
flags.get-credentials: Download kubeconfig for an AKS cluster
flags.--subscription: Subscription name or ID to use
flags.--output: Output format: json, jsonc, yaml, table, tsv, none
flags.--query: JMESPath query string to filter output
flags.--no-wait: Do not wait for long-running operations to finish
flags.--debug: Show detailed debug and HTTP request logs

args.description: Service group and command (e.g., gcloud compute instances list)

flags.init: Initialise gcloud config — set account, project, region
flags.auth: Manage authentication credentials and service accounts
flags.config: View and set gcloud configuration properties
flags.compute: Manage Google Compute Engine VMs, disks, and networks
flags.storage: Manage Google Cloud Storage buckets and objects (gsutil successor)
flags.run: Deploy and manage Cloud Run services (serverless containers)
flags.iam: Manage IAM policies, roles, and service accounts
flags.projects: List, create, and manage GCP projects
flags.services: Enable, disable, and list GCP APIs and services
flags.logging: Read and manage Cloud Logging log entries
flags.monitoring: Manage Cloud Monitoring dashboards and alert policies
flags.builds: Submit and manage Cloud Build jobs
flags.artifacts: Manage Artifact Registry repositories and packages
flags.dns: Manage Cloud DNS zones and records
flags.login: Authenticate with a Google account via browser
flags.activate-service-account: Authenticate using a service account key file
flags.ssh: SSH into a Compute Engine instance
flags.scp: Copy files to/from a Compute Engine instance
flags.get-credentials: Fetch kubeconfig credentials for a GKE cluster
flags.--account: Google account or service account to use
flags.--format: Output format: json, yaml, table, csv, text, none
flags.--verbosity: Logging verbosity: debug, info, warning, error, critical, none
flags.--impersonate-service-account: Run as a service account (requires iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator)
flags.--log-http: Log all HTTP requests and responses (for debugging)

|.name: Pipe
|.description: Passes stdout of the left command as stdin to the right command

>.name: Redirect stdout
>.description: Redirects stdout to a file, overwriting it

>>.name: Append stdout
>>.description: Redirects stdout to a file, appending to it

2>&1.name: Merge stderr→stdout
2>&1.description: Redirects stderr to the same destination as stdout

&.name: Background
&.description: Runs the command in the background

&&.name: AND operator
&&.description: Runs the right command only if the left command succeeds (exit 0)

||.name: OR operator
||.description: Runs the right command only if the left command fails (non-zero exit)

;.name: Command separator
;.description: Runs commands sequentially regardless of exit status
